observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct both the Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:

Maintain updated inventory records based on the receipt, acceptance, and issuance of inventory items as prescribed by the NGAS Manual;
Strictly follow the procedures set forth in Section 122 of the NGAS Manual; and
Conduct semestral (or oftener depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory items to be reported in the Report on Physical Count of Inventories which should be submitted to the Audit Team.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider allocating a room or space for stockroom of inventory items purchased.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned offices will  maintain updated inventory records based on the receipt, acceptance, and issuance of inventory items.

The Management will strictly follow the procedures set forth in Section 122 of the NGAS Manual.

The Inventory Committee will conduct semestral physical count of inventory items.

A stockroom has been allocated for inventories.
observations.reason: Although updated records are being prepared and a stockroom allocated, the Municipality is still using the expense method in recording inventory issuances; semi-annual physical count was not conducted as scheduled; stock cards and supplies ledger cards were not updated as of validation date; monthly SSMI not submitted by GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to:
ensure that the under-obligated amount of ₱165,227,160.00 is not carried forward to the CY 2025 SCBAA under continuing appropriations and revise the SCBAA to reflect the changes;
thereafter, fully obligate the total amount of all perfected contracts under the Actual Amounts column of the SCBAA to ensure the faithful representation of the Municipality’s financial position, in accordance with applicable accounting standards and budgetary principles, to enhance the accuracy of reported budget utilization and promote better transparency and accountability in financial reporting; and
conduct monthly or quarterly reconciliation of records to support the accurate preparation of year-end reports and ensure the reliability of information presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
accurately reflect in the original budget column of the current year’s SCBAA the ending balances of previous year’s CO in accordance with IPSAS No. 24 and Section 322 of RA No. 7160; and
strengthen validation procedures during the finalization of year-end reports by performing a reconciliation between the SAAOB and the SCBAA to ensure the accuracy and consistency of reported balances and to avoid the same oversight in the succeeding year.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the completed projects from the CIP account to the appropriate PPE accounts, as follows:
update the CIP monitoring report to accurately identify projects that have been completed; and
thereafter, establish a practice of timely reclassification of completed projects to the appropriate asset accounts.
Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with Certificates of Completion for projects completed as of year-end to facilitate their accurate recording.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
make the necessary adjusting entry to present fairly the carrying amount of the PPE accounts in the books of the Municipality, as follows:
ensure that, moving forward, all PPE are provided with appropriate depreciation to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
effect the proposed adjusting journal entry, as follows:
restate the financial statements to reflect the proposed adjusting entry.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
GSO-Designate to submit to the Municipal Accountant all necessary supporting documents, including delivery receipts, inspection and acceptance reports, and other relevant papers, to ensure the accurate recording of accrued expenditures for goods and services delivered within the year.
Municipal Accountant to:
effect the proposed adjusting entry as follows:
restate the Statement of Financial Position to reflect the proposed adjusting entry; and
hereafter, strictly observe the proper recognition of AP by recording liabilities only upon actual delivery of goods or rendering of services based on the necessary corroborating documents of the transaction, pursuant to the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume III.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the balances of the affected receivables and their corresponding contra-accounts, as proposed below:
initiate and expedite the write-off process by following the required procedures and ensuring the timely submission of all required documentation to the Audit Team for validation, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the LCE discontinue the practice of issuing cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer, unless a specific, well-defined, and properly documented purpose has been clearly established, ensuring greater accountability and transparency in financial transactions and to uphold sound cash management practices.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
facilitate the disbursement of recurring labor payroll expenses through ATM transactions to enhance efficiency and convenience; and
expedite the reconciliation of records to accurately identify the causes of unliquidated cash advance balances, enforce the prompt liquidation of any remaining amounts, and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the LCE discontinue the practice of issuing cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer, unless a specific, well-defined, and properly documented purpose has been clearly established, ensuring greater accountability and transparency in financial transactions and to uphold sound cash management practices.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
facilitate the disbursement of recurring labor payroll expenses through ATM transactions to enhance efficiency and convenience; and
expedite the reconciliation of records to accurately identify the causes of unliquidated cash advance balances, enforce the prompt liquidation of any remaining amounts, and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the applicable taxes on all accrued expenses and remit the same to the BIR within the prescribed period in compliance with RR No. 04-2024.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Engineer to:
expedite the completion of the laboratory unit and ensure that all necessary facility requirements, including the installation of the specialized door for the X-ray room, are complied with to facilitate the immediate installation and operation of the digital X-ray machine; and
hereafter, coordinate closely with the concerned agencies, such as the Department of Health, during the planning and pre-procurement stages to ensure full compliance with infrastructure and regulatory requirements prior to the procurement of highly specialized equipment.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
remit the remaining balance of the Due to BIR account without further delay to avoid the incurrence of penalties, surcharges, and other legal consequences; and
establish a structured and continuous process for the review and monitoring of subsidiary ledgers related to withheld taxes to prevent lapses, ensure regulatory compliance, and safeguard the integrity of financial operations.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MENRO, in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer, to provide training for the garbage collectors and other personnel directly dealing with the collection of solid wastes in compliance with Section 23 (b) of RA No. 9003, otherwise consider the TESDA Garbage Collector NC I training program to equip them with the necessary skills on proper handling of waste and avoid exposure to possible health risks and injuries.

direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Cards and PPELC, respectively, for each class of PPE as required in NGAS for LGUs by at least starting with those recently acquired through Acceptance and Inspection Reports, Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE), Disbursement Vouchers, supporting documents and other reports.

B. Non-compliance in the accounting and reporting procedures of Local Roads Asset Management System

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:

The Municipal Accountant to:
trace and duly account local road network transactions prior to CY 2016 through journals, disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports;
thereafter, provide the necessary data to the Municipal Engineer and GSO in the conduct of physical inventory of local roads;
keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components based on the data to be provided by the Municipal Engineer for every road network; and
provide depreciation expense for each of the component; and
Disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for Local Road Network;
The General Services Officer to:
prepare and maintain the required Local Road Networks Property Card and include in the conduct of inventory the Local Road Networks;
prepare and submit Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
The Municipal Engineer to provide the Local Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to accurately reflect in the Original Budget column of the current year’s SCBAA the current and continuing appropriation ending balances of previous year’s capital outlay; and
Municipal Budget Officer to revert to the General Fund the unutilized balance of MOOE amounting to ₱11,629,724.00 and initiate a supplemental budget through an appropriation ordinance or law to authorize the re-appropriation of such PPA before utilization.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution authorizing the transfer of Barangay Health Centers to the various recipient barangays, and then prepare Deeds of Donation for the actual transfer and acceptance by the latter to substantiate the transfer of ownership of the said properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee who shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan containing the specific assignments/duties of the committee members, the cut-off date and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start to finish.
Thereafter, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Inventory Committee, GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to undertake the procedural guidelines enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to come up with more reliable account balances to be presented in the financial statements.
Lastly, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation for the Local Chief Executive to direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to prepare Property and Equipment Cards and PPELC, respectively, for each class of PPE as required in NGAS for LGUs by at least starting with those recently acquired through Acceptance and Inspection Reports, Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE), Disbursement Vouchers, supporting documents and other reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct both the Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain updated inventory records based on the receipt, acceptance, and issuance of inventory items as prescribed by the NGAS Manual;
Strictly follow the procedures set forth in Section 122 of the NGAS Manual; and
Conduct semestral (or oftener depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory items to be reported in the RPCI which should be submitted to the Audit Team.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider allocating a room or space for stockroom of inventory items purchased.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to exercise utmost prudence and diligence in her duties and responsibilities in ensuring that all pertinent documents as discussed above are attached in the disbursement vouchers in adherence to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 prior the processing of payment of all infrastructure projects and to substantiate the legality, validity, and propriety of the said transactions; and
The Municipal Engineer, as the focal person for all infrastructure projects, to submit contracts and necessary supporting documents particularly the As-Shown plans, breakdown of ABC, Bill of Quantities/ Detailed Estimates and other necessary documents to the office of the auditor within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Implementing/End-User Units to prepare the PPMPs in accordance with Section 7 of IRR of RA No. 9184 and avoid the use of brand names in the items to be procured by including the technical specifications;
The Budget Officer to thoroughly review and evaluate the submitted PPMPs in terms of amount and its coherence with the proposed budget of the LGU by issuing a standard pricing system of budget cost for similar items in order to attain the most advantageous price during the procurement; and
The BAC through the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMPs and prepare the APP with complete schedule of each procurement activity not only for procurement of goods but also for infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the BAC Chairman to monitor each procurement activity specified in the APP and reflect the information in the Procurement Monitoring Report in the form prescribed by the GPBB.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the HRMO to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to formulate internal policies and procedures on the evaluation and approval of salary loan applications of employees; and submit to the LCE for her approval. Thereafter, implement the approved policies and procedures and encourage employees to adhere thereon; and
Prepare payslips and distribute the same, by office, to every employee to serve as personal record of earnings and deductions for the period they have rendered service.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to assign a Payroll Officer to the Human Resources Management Office who should prepare and process the payrolls of the Municipality to ensure the proper segregation of duties and lessen the workload of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to work closely with the PGLU to streamline the process of aggregating data into the eRPTS system by developing protocols for the timely transfer of assessment rolls and other relevant data from PGLU to MAssO and MTO to ensure consistency and accuracy in RPT reporting.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to make a representation with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to seek assistance in the enforcement of collection of delinquent taxes, especially those long over-due.
Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive ask for the assistance of the Barangay Officials in informing the heirs of the deceased delinquent taxpayers or occupants of the properties to pay their delinquent taxes.

a. FY2020 Shares from RA No. 7171 and RA No. 8240 totaling ₱265,413,544.50 received during the year were not included in the Actual Amounts column for Revenue from External Source.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to present the received amount of FY2020 Shares from RA No. 7171 and RA No. 8240 totaling ₱265,413,544.50 under the Actual Amounts column for Revenue from External Sources in the SCBAA.9

b. Changes in the budgetary requirements for MOOE and CO in the 20% LDF were not reflected in the SCBAA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:

The Municipal Accountant to present the a.) amounts approved in the Annual Budget in the Original Budgeted Amounts column; b) changes in the original budget resulting from the reprogramming of continuing appropriations in the Final Budgeted Amounts column; 10

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to use the appropriate account classification of the PPAs during the budget preparation and before the enactment of the budget.11

observations.recommendation:
With the foregoing, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Municipal Engineer to furnish copies of Certificates of Project Completion to the Municipal Accountant upon completion of the project as basis of the Accountant to recognize the completed projects in the books; and
The Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the completed projects amounting ₱74,274,805.00 as per PPA Report and Certificates of Completion.  Thereafter, recognize all succeeding completed projects based on the PPA report, Certificate of Completion and other documents to be provided by the Municipal Engineer.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the accounts as presented above and thereafter instruct her to account transactions accordingly based on their actual nature using applicable accounting laws, rules and regulations; and
instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the classification of programs, projects, and activities during budget preparation to ensure that financial reports will reflect the actual intent of Management for these programs.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Food Supplies Expense and Welfare Goods for Distribution accounts by ₱1,033,890.00 and subsequently, apply perpetual inventory method in accounting for supplies and materials as prescribed by New Government Accounting System Manual for Local Government Units;
instruct all requesting departments to prepare Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) for every request for release of supplies and materials in stock and require the General Services Officer to consolidate all issuances through Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted together with all RIS to the Municipal Accountant as her basis in properly accounting issuances of inventory; and
direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to maintain stock cards and Supplies Ledger Card, respectively, to monitor movement of inventory and ensure that these records are regularly reconciled to ensure all inventory transactions are duly accounted for.

The Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers for CY 2019 in order to restate the prior year’s affected accounts in the Comparative Financial Statements with appropriate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
The Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant as part of internal rules establish cut-off procedures particularly during year-end closing of books of accounts to ensure that all revenues and expenditures are recognized on the correct period/year.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin:
the Municipal Accountant to trace the noted differences from the audited and recorded balances and make necessary adjusting entries and to regularly update her records in order to verify immediately the amount to be remitted based on her records; and
the Municipal Treasurer to validate if the unremitted contributions indicated in the PHIC’s Statement of Premium Account were not yet paid and if proven to be unremitted, to pay those unpaid premiums so as to avoid penalties and charges and to monitor the remittance of monthly contributions withheld from the salaries and wages of officials and employees.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to religiously prepare and maintain journals and ledgers and timely submit Interim Financial Statements that would extremely aid in the decision making of the Management.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to train her staff with knowledge and skills deemed necessary, or if the budget permits, hire additional staff in the accounting office that would help in the preparation of journals and ledgers; and
Require all department heads to duly accomplish all documents and reports with all the necessary information that would facilitate a smooth and timely recording of financial transactions by the Municipal Accountant.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to religiously review her records and certify cash availability in the appropriate account prior preparation and issuance of check to ensure that funds will only be disbursed on the purpose for which they were appropriated.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to trace transactions related to Due to NGAs- RA 7171 Congressional Shares through disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the period covering 2018 and 2019 with the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer to establish the correct amount of remaining savings available for appropriation.
Thereafter, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books to close the Due to NGAs and CIP accounts while recognizing the appropriate PPE account and increase in Government Equity.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Secretariat to post and upload NOA and Contract/Purchase Order of all awarded contracts of goods and supplies for Covid-19 to promote accountability and transparency in the conduct of Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act. Additionally, to strictly comply with all the mandatory documentary requirements under the GPPB Circular No. 001-2020 and submit to this office the lacking documents for further review on the legality of the transactions.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
All departments and requesting offices to conduct adequate and judicious planning so that procurement of the needed items could be done timely and in compliance to RA No. 9184.
All department heads to completely and properly filled-out PPMPs as useful basis of BAC in the consolidation of APP;
Requesting offices to initiate the preparation of the Purchase Request and OBR within a reasonable time in order to instigate the whole procurement activity before the scheduled events; and
Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that all implementation of programs/activities had passed through the appropriate procurement activity based on the mode of procurement employed as required under RA No. 9184.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider using available funds from savings of previous years’ appropriations first through appropriation ordinances, which amounts to at least ₱8,953,170.57, for future additional programs and projects deemed necessary, such as those related to the COVID-19 pandemic since there is an extension of State of Calamity, before realigning those planned in the current year’s annual budget.
Moreover, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Budget Officer and Accountant to maintain the registries and other prepare at least every quarter reports such as SAAOB and Financial Statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Development Council to judiciously plan the PPAs to be funded under the 20% Development Fund to optimize the projects to be implemented to avoid recurring projects that caters only to certain beneficiaries. Likewise, invest on infrastructure projects/equipment or any other capital expenditures in allocating its funds to cater all its constituents to enjoy social, economic and environmental development essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to include the mandatory details in the preparation of Program of Work, and the Municipal Accountant to assure that the required supporting documents are submitted to facilitate assessment of the reasonableness of the cost and duration of implementation of projects.

We have recommended that Management submit documents and justifications to support the propriety of the hazard pay claims and revisit the guidelines and conditions on the grant of the COVID-19 Hazard Pay issued by the Department of Budget and Management to avoid future infractions. Further, for the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prior to payment of claims.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Engineer to:
Closely coordinate with barangays in the monitoring of construction/improvement of buildings with no permit;
Monitor the issuance of building and occupancy permits; and
Furnish the Assessor’s Office of the issued building permits within 30 days from their issuance.
Municipal Assessor to:
Conduct field inspection;
Declare and assess real properties based on their actual use;
Use the approved CLUP in the reclassification of agricultural lands; and
Provide the Treasury Office of the Assessment Roll every December 31.
We further recommended that management conduct tax campaign in order to educate the Municipality’s constituents on their responsibilities as taxpayers.
Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reflect in the book of accounts the distribution of Farm Equipment amounting to ₱30,710,800.00 and;

observations.recommendation:

Moreover, cause the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory for all class of PPEs as recorded, and immediately reconcile records with the Inventory Report to ensure that all PPE transactions are accurately and completely recorded in the book of accounts.
Therefrom, immediately update PPE records for every PPE transaction incurred and regularly conduct timely physical inventory and reconciliation of PPE records to ensure that all PPEs are properly accounted for.
Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reflect in the book of accounts the distribution of Farm Equipment amounting to ₱30,710,800.00 and further trace unreconciled balance amounting ₱3,241,650.12 between her ledgers and the Financial Statements.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in her books to reflect adequate accumulated depreciation for each class of identified PPE in the incoming year. Thereafter, ensure that there will be adequate recognition of depreciation for each class of PPE using PPELCs in the succeeding years to reflect a more reliable financial information on PPEs for users of the Financial Statements.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Municipal Accountant to trace and duly account local road network transactions prior to CY 2016 through journals, disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports. Thereafter, provide the necessary data to the Municipal Engineer and GSO in the conduct of physical inventory of local roads;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components based on the data to be provided by the Municipal Engineer for every road network; provide depreciation expense for each of the component; and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for Local Road Network;
The General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the required Local Road Networks Property Card and include in the conduct of inventory the Local Road Networks;
The General Services Officer to prepare and submit Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
The Municipal Engineer to provide the Local Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all the cash in bank accounts. Moreover, trace, verify, and reconcile variances presented between the cash records. Thereafter, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, exert more time and effort in reconciling regularly the book balances against the bank records including reconciliation with the cashbooks to ensure all cash transactions are appropriately accounted for in the book of accounts.

Thereafter, ensure that all liability control accounts are duly supported with updated subsidiary ledgers.

Otherwise, disqualify future bids whose key personnel and equipment are already pledged / used in other on-going projects to avoid unnecessary delays in the completion of projects.

Instead, require Schools to attach schedule of materials they needed in their request for assistance from the Municipality that will serve as basis for Management in the procurement of the said materials.

We have recommended that the management devise strategies and control mechanisms to address the cause of the delay. Thereafter, ensure that transaction documents and financial reports are submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Establish Petty Cash Fund for emergency and petty operating expenses consisting of small payments for maintenance and operating expenses which cannot be paid conveniently by check.  Replenish the same with the actual amount of expenditures when it already reached at least 75% of the amount established by submitting a replenishment voucher with all supporting documents duly summarized in a report of disbursements;
Separate the cash advance for specific activities such as payment of salaries and wages of Job Orders, labor payroll and miscellaneous operating activities from the advances for operating expenses; and
Make direct payment through check to the suppliers for transactions exceeding P15,000.00.
Further, the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of the supporting documents prior to processing of the Disbursement Vouchers.
Lastly, the Bids and Awards Committee make timely and proper procurement plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to recognize the property, plant and equipment and income;
Record the said receipts and LGU Equity under the Trust Fund books; and
Maintain subsidiary ledgers to facilitate determination of fund balances per program/project/activity.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
transfer immediately the received funds and LGU Equity to a Trust Fund depository account; and
ensure that payments be made with the appropriate funds or cash advance to avoid juggling of funds.
Lastly, we recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7160 particularly on the establishment of Trust Fund to avoid reversion of unutilized balances of appropriations subject for reversion at the end of the year and that programs/projects/activities which are partly funded by other government agencies are not yet completed.  Further, appropriation must be made specific in order not to hamper or delay implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Accountant to maintain special account for Excise Tax Shares under the General Fund; and
Municipal Treasurer to see to it that funds from Share from Excise Tax be deposited on the bank account opened and maintained specifically intended for RA 7171.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct signatories of vouchers to sign and process transactions only when these are supported with duly certified and completely filled-up Obligation Request to ensure that all transactions of the LGU are appropriated and to avoid overdrafts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant committed to adjust the accounts affected to properly account the asset particularly the infrastructure accounts and income arising from the BUB transactions and RA No. 7171 Congressional Share.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
Submit Final Report or Completion Report together with the fund liquidation report to the NPCO/PSO/RPCO;
Return unutilized funds to Department of Agriculture in the amounts of P10,000.00 and P30,000.00 for GOP and Loan Proceeds counterparts, respectively and General Fund in the amount of P159,803.74 in order to avoid additional service charge and not to hamper implementation of General Fund programs/projects/activities; and
Strictly comply with the Memorandum of Agreement or Implementation Management Agreement, particularly in the financial management system for future National Agency Funded programs/projects/activities in a manner adequate to reflect the operations and financial condition of the Municipality, including operations, resources and expenditures related to the Project, including recovery of all costs, for carrying out the Sub-Project or any part thereof.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive guaranteed to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the disbursement vouchers, payrolls and official receipts to the Auditor within ten (10) days from date of receipt of said documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to submit immediately the required interim financial statements together with the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations, journals and bank reconciliation statements.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant, Bids and Awards Committee and Focal Person to submit the lacking supporting documents.
We also recommended that management strictly implement the provisions of RA No. 9184 on all of its procurement.  It should judiciously prepare, maintain and update a Project Procurement Plan (PPMP) for each individual project to be consolidated in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP), which shall be the basis for procurement.
It is further recommended that the Municipality adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement and resort only to alternative methods in highly exceptional cases provided under RA No. 9184.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to revisit completed projects during the one year defects liability period and before the issuance of Final Certificate of Acceptance.  Further, to require contractors whose projects were one year completed to post warranty against structural defects from final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2.
We recommended that management submit the required necessary supporting documents in order to ascertain the validity and propriety of the payment of the payroll for job orders / contract of service.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer collect from the health workers, the excess payment of laundry allowance.

We recommended that the Human Resource officer prior to processing of the terminal leave benefits ensure completeness of the documents needed as provided in the above regulations of CSC on Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292 and COA Circ. No. 2012-01.
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the required supporting to documents to facilitate verification of the correctness of the BAC Honoraria.

We recommended that Management ensure that the basic elements or requirements must be incorporated in the consultancy contracts to clearly provide the duties and responsibilities of the consultants and the LGU.  Further, to assure the legality and regularity of the payment of consultancy services and reasonableness of the contract costs.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer integrate in the Program of Work the scope of work, the details of exact location and area covered by the clearing of canals, dredging and the like.  Further, to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents and thereafter, the Municipal Accountant must see to it that complete supporting documents are attached in the payroll.

We recommended that the Municipality thru the Municipal Social Welfare and Development (MSWD) Office adhere to the provisions of DSWD Department Order No. 22 in order to ensure the proper implementation of the program and ascertain the validity and propriety of the transaction.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required supporting documents for scholarship.

We recommended and Management committed to submit at least the Approved documents indicating the number of personnel involved and their corresponding salary and schedule of work and places of assignment or station/visits indicating, among others, the number of hours per visit.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the Inspection and Acceptance Report as required under COA Circular No. 2009-01.  Further, attach the accomplishment report and pictures “before, during and after” in the disbursement voucher.

We recommended that immediate installation of adequate controls for requisition of gasoline and other oil derivatives and approval of corresponding POs must be made through enforcement of necessary documents as required by existing rules and regulations such as:
Corresponding driver’s trip tickets and monthly report of official travels to support the use of government vehicles must be attached in the Disbursement Vouchers for payment of gasoline and oil derivative.
The supply officer must prepare reconciliation statement between the gasoline breakdown issued by the gasoline station / dealer and the supply officer’s record. Duplicate of requisition slip must be kept on file by the Supply Officer as one of his basis in preparing reconciliation statement.
A written agreement or contract with the gasoline dealer must be undertaken to formalize the terms and conditions as well as limitations/restrictions.

We recommended that immediate installation of adequate controls for requisition of gasoline and other oil derivatives and approval of corresponding POs must be made through enforcement of necessary documents as required by existing rules and regulations such as:
Corresponding driver’s trip tickets and monthly report of official travels to support the use of government vehicles must be attached in the Disbursement Vouchers for payment of gasoline and oil derivative.
The supply officer must prepare reconciliation statement between the gasoline breakdown issued by the gasoline station / dealer and the supply officer’s record. Duplicate of requisition slip must be kept on file by the Supply Officer as one of his basis in preparing reconciliation statement.
A written agreement or contract with the gasoline dealer must be undertaken to formalize the terms and conditions as well as limitations/restrictions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive committed to direct:
The Bids and Awards Committee to see to it that procurement documents must be properly and completely filled-up; and
The Municipal Accountant to delegate an Accounting personnel to check the completeness of the disbursement vouchers or payrolls prior to processing of payment.

We recommended and Management agreed that the buildings and other properties be insured with the GSIS.  Further, determine the fair market value of the buildings with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer and Assessor. Lastly, the premium pertaining to these must be included in the appropriation of the LGU.

We recommended that for future projects funded by the Government of the Philippines, the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Bids and Awards Committee to meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement and strictly observe the indicative procurement timelines; and
The Focal Person in-charge to coordinate closely with the donor agency to avoid delays in the implementation.

We recommended that the focal person closely coordinate with the MLGOO in reviewing the data gathered and screen for errors and duplication.
For future projects, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LGU focal person to closely coordinate with the donor agency and to prepare immediately the PPMP and submit the same to the Bids and Awards Committee for on time procurement.  Further, strict supervision must be undertaken in order to avoid errors and duplication especially for data based projects.

We recommended and Management committed to ensure monitoring the full implementation of the projects appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to address the needs of the constituents such as access to improved health, social, agriculture and economic services.  Further, for the Municipal Development Council to review prior years’ unimplemented projects under the 20% development fund and revise their Annual Investment Plan which are directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the municipality continue to appropriate at least 5% of their total annual budget for gender and development plans and programs to ensure the promotion of public welfare and basic services addressing gender issues.
The GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for CY 2017 of the municipality was found to be fully compliant in form and contents with the provisions of the JMC 2013-01: Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women (MCW).
The Provincial Planning and Development Coordinator of the Province of La Union issued a certification that the programs, projects and activities included in the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2017 of the municipality were found to be in accordance with the priorities of the Provincial Government of La Union.
Further, the OIC-Provincial Director of DILG-La Union Provincial Office certified that the Gender and Development Plan and Budget of the municipality was fully compliant in form and contents with the provisions of the JMC 2013-01: Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women (MCW).
We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management continue to identify programs and projects that address gender issues and concerns and promote gender equality and women empowerment. Likewise, to continue to prepare GAD Plan and Budget in accordance with JMC 2013-01: Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women (MCW).

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials and employees fully implement programs/projects/activities in accordance with DILG-approved Gad Plan and Budget to fully address gender issues and women empowerment of the constituents. Further, for the GFPS to ensure and monitor strict implementation of the annual GAD PPAs for the constituents to fully utilize the benefit that could be derived therefrom.
When necessary, to amend the plan and submit for approval by the approving agency.
We recommended that the GAD Focal person submit the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review of the same.

Municipal Accountant is still tracing previous years’ files for the said receivables.
The municipality has started coordinating with barangay officials to enforce laws and ordinances applicable within the barangay.

For 2018, the municipality had required each department to judiciously prepare, maintain and update a Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for each individual or end-user units to be consolidated in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP), which shall be the basis for procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management committed that the Local Finance Committee must see to it that the 20% Development Fund be appropriated and expended to development projects of the municipality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Department Heads and all officials and employees to submit list of money claims against the municipality with the required supporting documents and set cut-off date particularly at year-end.  Based on the submitted money claims, the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer will obligate the said expenses.  Thereafter, prior year’s expense will no longer be charged against the current year’s appropriations.

We recommended and management agreed that the Fidelity Bond of the above Accountable Officers be increased accordingly in compliance to Treasury Circular No. 1-99.  We recommended further that in order to avoid embezzlement or misappropriation of cash accountability, we encourage the collectors and Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit and deposit their collections.

The Municipal Treasurer assigned her Assistant to diligently monitor the remittances of the collectors daily and to deposit the same immediately on the next banking day.  Likewise, accountable forms will no longer be issued not unless all the collections were remitted by the collectors.
We have reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly supervise the collectors in remitting their collections.  We recommended further that for management impose administrative disciplinary action to collectors who violate the above provision of law.
Lastly, for the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections/remittances daily to their depository banks.

We recommended that in order to maximize the benefits of the iTAX system provided by the Provincial Government, the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor coordinate with the concerned officers or employees of the Provincial Government to check the installed iTAX system in the municipality.  We recommended further that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the collectors to completely fill-up the columns provided in the Accountable Form No. 56.

We have recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant committed to:
For the Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cashbook for each time deposit account; and
For the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each account and prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to book the municipal building and slaughterhouse for fair presentation of financial statements. We recommended further that representations be made by the Municipality to DPWH in order to be informed on the cost of the building since it was occupied in 2016.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause or order the Municipal Accountant to further look into previous years’ files in order to account for the said receivables.  If after all the possible means were exerted and the claims could not be accounted, request for write-off of the above receivables supported by above cited documents.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to release the barangay shares from real property taxes to the different barangays within five (5) days after the end of each quarter and shall not be subjected to any lien or holdback for whatever purpose in order that all the shares from real property taxes could be utilized by the barangays for the benefits of their constituents.

We have recommended and management agreed that in order to have an assurance that the Building Code of the Philippines is properly enforced within the Municipality and in order to increase collection from building permit fees, the Municipal Mayor will enjoin the Barangay Officials to enforce laws and ordinances applicable within the barangay and to coordinate with the Building Official when they notice that there are structures being built within their barangay.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly implement the provisions of RA No.  9184 on all its procurement of goods.  We further required each department to judiciously prepare, maintain and update a Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for each individual or end-user units to be consolidated in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP), which shall be the basis for procurement.
Further, we recommended to the BAC to adopt public bidding as a general mode of procurement and resort only to alternative methods in highly exceptional cases provided under the law. Also, to recommend to the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) through its issuance of BAC Resolution, the proper and correct alternative mode to be used in the procurement, the advertisements posted in the PhilGEPS or conspicuous place in the municipality.
Lastly, to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents and thereafter, the BAC and Municipal Accountant must see to it that all procurement transactions must be properly documented.

The team recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee committed to submit the performance security of the above projects.  Further, to ensure that all contracts entered into are supported with performance securities to guarantee faithful performance of by the contractor.

We have recommended that the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant properly observe Section 9.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and for the Engineering Office to comply with Annex “A” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 to ensure that actual projects implemented by the Municipal Government conform to the duly approved plans/drawings.
Further, to submit the above lacking documents to support the payment of infrastructure projects.

The audit team recommended that a signboard or billboard as required by the above Circulars be posted for all PPAs of the municipality with the essential features/information to ensure transparency and encourage public participation to the activities of the municipality. Further, for the agency to provide a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and to notify the audit team on the posting of the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices for the purpose of validation and verification.

We have recommended and management have committed that the Head of the Procuring entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Project Monitoring Report and approved by her and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended that the total funds received from PhilHealth in the amount of P706,132.66 be deposited in the bank account of the municipality under the Trust Fund books together with the capitation fund.  Further, the Municipal Accountant, to record the cash and payable accounts under the Trust Fund books of the municipality and to maintain subsidiary ledgers to separately account the receipts, disbursements and balances of each of the PhilHealth benefits received by the municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Health Officer coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee inorder for the transactions relating to procurement pass through the proper procurement process and complete supporting documents be attached to the Disbursement Vouchers.

We have recommended that the Municipal Health Officer conduct thorough consultation among the health staff and draft guidelines on the distribution of TB DOTS to be approved by the Local Chief Executive.

We have recommended that the municipality must reflect in the disbursement vouchers, supporting and bidding documents the actual cost of the rice seeds for transparency.  Thereafter, collection from farmers must be properly receipted and recorded for accountability.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to implement the Municipality’s funded Pre-Disaster Activities and projects to reduce the impact of calamity in accordance to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and to require the MDRRMC to monitor and evaluate its implementation.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Local Finance Committee determine the income reasonably collectible for the ensuing year taking into consideration the collection performance in the previous years in order to prevent bloated budget.

We have recommended that Management ensure that funds allocated for Special Education Fund be budgeter and utilized strictly in accordance with Section 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG, series of 1998, 2000 and 2001 to effectively attain the purpose for which the fund was created. The above expenditures may be appropriated to the General Fund.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity, direct the BAC and Municipal Accountant to submit the above lacking supporting documents immediately. Thereafter, prior to payment, the Municipal Accountant and BAC must ensure complete supporting documents in order to prove that vital procurement procedures or activities were properly undertaken.
